title: dryopteris thelypteris subsp. pubescens
intro: Dryopteris thelypteris subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy male fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The hairy male fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of the hairy male fern include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote healing, making it a valuable treatment for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the hairy male fern is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
